metadata.title: EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) Compliance Timeline for AppSec Tools
metadata.source: Official Journal of the European Union

key_dates.description: Published as Regulation (EU) 2024/2847 in the Official Journal
Member states must notify conformity assessment bodies
Manufacturers must report actively exploited vulnerabilities and severe incidents to ENISA
key_dates.impact: Critical - All AppSec vendors must have vulnerability reporting pipelines

key_dates.description: All CRA requirements become mandatory for products with digital elements
key_dates.impact: Full compliance required - SBOM, vulnerability handling, security updates

compliance_requirements.description: Manufacturers must identify and document vulnerabilities including open-source components
Machine-readable SBOM identifying all components in products
Ensure timely security updates are available and delivered to users
Report actively exploited vulnerabilities within 24 hours, full report within 72 hours
Products must be delivered with secure default settings
Products must undergo conformity assessment based on risk category
Establish policies and procedures for coordinated vulnerability disclosure

open_source_steward_obligations.description: Open source stewards (foundations, maintainers of widely-used FOSS) have lighter obligations
key_requirements: Establish cybersecurity policy for vulnerability handling
Support downstream manufacturers with security information

penalties.non_compliance_essential: Up to EUR 15 million or 2.5% of worldwide annual turnover
penalties.non_compliance_other: Up to EUR 10 million or 2% of worldwide annual turnover
penalties.incorrect_information: Up to EUR 5 million or 1% of worldwide annual turnover
